refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how latency is achieved, only that it must be compatible with safe operation. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety requirement without prescribing a specific latency value or technology, maintaining problem domain focus.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria defined by stakeholders, such as latency p95 ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms to maintain latency within threshold
Separate teleoperation control commands from non-critical data streams to reduce communication load
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules could increase coupling
Shared data buffers or synchronous processing might create dependencies
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a DP focused on system architecture and data flow allows modular separation of communication and control, supporting independence and easier latency management.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Real-time processing capabilities are sufficient to handle data without introducing delays
Predictive control algorithms may be needed if latency approaches threshold, adding complexity
Monitoring mechanisms must be accurate and responsive to detect latency spikes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency monitoring and control mechanisms in the DP to ensure continuous compliance with latency requirements under varying network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering strategies across the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time data streams for processing and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange with external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is designed to handle core data processing; minimal diffusion as buffering and real-time processing are central to this module.
Vehicle module handles physical operations; real-time data processing is peripheral, leading to moderate diffusion.
Teleoperation requires some real-time data but focuses on control commands, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, less on data processing, leading to weak containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP of real-time data processing and minimal buffering due to its central role in system architecture and data handling.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation for control-related data processing may cause interface complexity.
If real-time processing requirements evolve to include vehicle-level processing, diffusion may increase.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate control data processing from general real-time data processing to better align with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving low end-to-end communication latency necessary for safe teleoperation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module for control-related data processing may cause interface complexity and diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 8) and low diffusion (score 2) for the DP, indicating good containment. However, moderate diffusion in Teleoperation and Vehicle modules suggests some responsibilities related to real-time data processing are spread, posing a diffusion risk if not carefully managed.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through latency measurements (p95 ≤ 150 ms) is feasible given the clear mapping to real-time data processing in the NGHE Solution module and its interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces supporting the required data flows for teleoperation latency requirements.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage the interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to minimize diffusion risk.
Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate control data processing (Teleoperation) from general real-time data processing (NGHE Solution) if diffusion risks increase.
Regularly verify latency performance against acceptance criteria to ensure architectural decisions meet FR requirements.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mappings, feasible verification, and appropriate allocation of design parameters to implementation modules. Although there are some diffusion risks related to interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules, these are manageable through monitoring and refinement rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining architectural clarity and verification focus is prioritized over deeper decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate control data processing (Teleoperation) from general real-time data processing (NGHE Solution) if diffusion risks increase, to reduce interface complexity and diffusion risk.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR with respect to latency requirements.
Verification through latency measurements is feasible and reliable.
Interface complexity can be managed through monitoring and refinement without further decomposition.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion risk between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules may increase interface complexity.
If diffusion risk is not managed, it could impact system safety and performance.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data (p95 ≤ 150 ms) from system tests.
Interface complexity metrics between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules over time.
Verification reports confirming architectural decisions meet FR requirements.
